# Assistant/Associate/Full Professor of Biomedical Sciences (Tenure Track)
...
Employer Old Dominion University Location Norfolk, Virginia (US) Salary Salary will be competitive and accompanied by comprehensive benefits and a strong start-up package. Closing date Sep 10, 2026
...
## Job Details
...
The Department of Biomedical and Translational Sciences (BTS), Macon and Joan Brock Virginia Health Sciences Eastern Virginia Medical School at Old Dominion University (ODU) seeks exceptional applicants in its research division for two tenure-track / tenured faculty positions at the ASSISTANT and ASSOCIATE or FULL PROFESSOR level. Located in Norfolk’s historic Ghent neighborhood, the institution offers a highly collaborative academic environment with access to major hospital partners, extensive core facilities, and broad research infrastructure.
...
The successful candidate will join a collegial department of over 50 faculty members across its Research and Education Divisions. We are seeking individuals with expertise in inflammation, epigenetics and genomics, metabolism or systems biology to complement departmental strengths in cancer, cardiovascular and pulmonary disease, neuroscience and women’s and infant’s health. We are particularly interested in outstanding candidates who can thrive in a transdisciplinary research environment and can collaborate with faculty in the Center for Integrative Neuroscience and Inflammatory Diseases, the Leroy T. Canoles Jr. Cancer Center and the newly established Brock Institute for Nutrition Science and Health.
...
BTS faculty have access to a centralized biorepository, histological services, state-of-the-art proteomics and genomics facilities, data analytic infrastructure, and collaborative interactions with faculty in clinical departments and across the ODU campus, enabling strong integration of basic, translational and clinical research.
...
Successful applicants will conduct transformative basic and translational research that advances scientific discovery and enhances human health, have a strong track record of publications, proven or high potential for sustained extramural funding, and a clear commitment to collaboration.
...
Develop or maintain an extramurally funded research program. Teach and mentor medical and graduate students. Contribute to departmental, institutional, and professional service.
...
Qualifications: Candidates for Assistant Professor must hold a PhD, MD or MD/PhD, have relevant postdoctoral research experience, a strong record of publication, and exceptional potential to develop an externally funded research program. Candidates for Associate or Full Professor should demonstrate an established, extramurally funded research program, a strong publication record, and national recognition in their field.
...
Rank and salary: The successful applicant will hold a full-time academic appointment in the BTS department at a rank commensurate with qualifications and experience. Salary will be competitive and accompanied by comprehensive benefits and a strong start-up package.
...

results.title: Old Dominion University Researchers Define AI’s Role in Transportation Nationwide | Old Dominion University
highlights:
Old Dominion University Researchers Define AI’s Role in Transportation Nationwide | Old Dominion University
...
# Old Dominion University Researchers Define AI’s Role in Transportation Nationwide
...
Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly used to solve real-world problems, improving efficiency, strengthening decision-making and addressing complex challenges that affect everyday life.
...
At Old Dominion University, researchers across disciplines are applying AI to develop solutions that benefit communities and society. In Fall 2025, the University debuted MonarchSphere, the nation’s first-of-its-kind AI innovation and enablement ecosystem for higher education powered by Google Cloud. Through a centralized and scalable platform, faculty, researchers, students and institutional teams can access advanced AI capabilities, cloud infrastructure and collaborative innovative environments designed to support teaching and learning, research enablement, workforce development, operational transformation, and applied AI experimentation across disciplines. The University’s leadership in AI is gaining attention from industries, like transportation, that need help solving everyday life challenges.
...
Backed by a $500,000 award from the Transportation Research Board, a division of the U.S. National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine, the team will study how AI will reshape the transportation workforce. The research will explore how AI may affect public-sector transportation jobs, how roles and responsibilities could evolve, and what new skills will be required.
...
investigators Murat Kuzlu
...
To address that need, the team will create a guidebook to help transportation agencies use AI safely, while preparing employees for changing roles.
...
The two-year project will include focus groups and surveys of transportation agencies nationwide to understand how AI is currently being used, how it is affecting employees’ daily responsibilities and where gaps exist. They will then identify tasks that can be supported or augmented by AI, outline the skills workers will need and develop practical recommendations for agencies to adopt AI, while supporting their workforce.
...
is already being used in
...
monitor road conditions,
...
assets, such as signs
...
For engineers and transportation professionals, generative AI tools are being used to quickly scan through long manuals and technical documents, helping the experts quickly find the information they need.
...
Dr. Tomovic said in many cases, transportation agencies are already facing staffing shortages, making AI a potential tool
...
help fill gaps.
...
The team says AI is more likely to change jobs than replace them. Most work in the transportation industry, they say, will still require human judgment, especially in complex decisions involving safety, design and funding. In those cases, AI will act as a tool to support workers, not replace them.
...
As AI continues to reshape industries, the transportation sector stands at a pivotal moment — one that requires both innovation and careful preparation. The work led by Old Dominion University researchers highlights the importance of not only advancing AI technologies, but also equipping the workforce to use them effectively and responsibly.
...
By developing a practical roadmap for adoption, the team’s research will help transportation agencies navigate rapid technological change, while maintaining safety, accountability and public trust. It reinforces a critical message: the future of transportation will not be defined by AI alone, but by how people and technology evolve together.
...
Through this effort and initiatives like MonarchSphere, Old Dominion University is positioning itself at the forefront of applied AI, driving solutions that extend beyond campus and into the communities these systems serve.

# Senior AI Developer
...
Join a forward-thinking university team shaping the future of research with cutting-edge AI. As a Senior AI Developer, you’ll design and deploy secure, custom solutions powered by Large Language Models and RAG pipelines—helping researchers work smarter, faster, and more efficiently. From building high-performance vector databases to advancing ethical, institutionally grounded AI, your work will directly support innovation at scale. If you’re a skilled Python developer with hands-on LLM experience and a passion for turning complex technology into real-world impact, this is your opportunity to lead meaningful change in a collaborative academic environment.
...
- 
- The Senior AI Developer will be a member of the Office of the Vice President for Research & Innovation (OVPRI) IT team and is responsible for the design, development, and maintenance of customized artificial intelligence solutions that enhance the research mission of the University. The Developer will leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to create secure tools that will accelerate and enhance the research administration process. The incumbent must balance high-level machine learning engineering with a deep commitment to digital ethics and institutional data sovereignty.
...
### LLM Development and Managmeent-
...
- 
- 

- Custom LLM Implementation: Design and maintain LLM models that allows the research community to access models via a secure, authenticated internal interface.

- RAG Pipeline Construction: Build and optimize Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines that allow AI agents to "read" university-specific research administration and regulatory affairs data (grant proposals, contracts, agreements, protocols, etc) without leaking that data into public training sets.

- Vector Database Management: Manage and scale vector databases to ensure high-speed semantic search across research administration and regulatory affairs documents.

- New AI
...
- 
- 

- Document LLM and RAG processes and configurations

- Document APIs to allow applications to utilize AI technologies developed

- Provide recommendations on appropriate training documents for models and fine-time parameters.

- Provide detailed training documentation to users as needed.
...
### Research AI Technologies -
...
- 
- 

- Research new AI technologies and provide recommendations on implementation.

- Test new AI methodologies and usages and provide recommendations for integration with existing systems.
...
### Minimum Qualifications
...
- Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

- Minimum of 2 years of experience in software development with at least 1 year of hands-on experience developing applications using LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or open-source models); or an equivalent combination of education and professional experience.
...
- Advanced proficiency in Python, including experience with asynchronous programming and data manipulation libraries (Pandas, vLLM, LangChain, PyTorch).
...
- Demonstrated experience building with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architectures and orchestration tools (like document chunking, embedding, and retrieval inferences)
...
- Experience with at least one Vector Database (e.g. Chroma, Qdrant).
...
- Ability to translate
...
### Preferred Qualifications
...
- 
- 

- Subspeciality or Degree in a field related to Artificial Intelligence.

- Previous experience working within a Higher Education environment.

- Experience utilizing an HPC cluster (slurm/ bash scripts and GPU usage)

- Knowledge of PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning) methods, specifically LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)

- Basic ability to build rapid prototypes to demonstrate AI tools to users.

results.title: ODU Researchers Decreased Students’ Intimidation of Writing Intensive Courses and Improved Outcomes | Old Dominion University
highlights:
ODU Researchers Decreased Students’ Intimidation of Writing Intensive Courses and Improved Outcomes | Old Dominion University
...
Researchers at Old Dominion University are testing how to decrease that intimidation by incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) tools at key points in the course. Their findings: more students succeeded.
...
Dr. Brown has spent the past three years refining a writing-intensive undergraduate course designed to improve both student confidence and success rates. With assistance from Joint School of Public Health colleagues Maria Kronenberg, Ph.D. (MBA ’90, Ph.D. ’00), Ashlee Steeley, D.H.Sc. (D.H.Sc. ’26), and recent graduate Diana Tagbor (B.S.P.H. ’25), the course blended structured teaching methods, iterative feedback and carefully guided use of AI. The idea came to Dr. Brown after she attended a conference in 2024, where it was clear that the concern about AI and writing went far beyond her students. In response, PUBH 403W: Social and Behavioral Aspects of Public Health was redesigned around a semester-long research project where students analyze a real community health issue, identify a target population, design an intervention and ultimately evaluate whether their program would work.
...
While the assignment isn’t new to public health, the approach of incorporating AI into the structure of this particular class is.
...
“It’s about helping students tell a story with data,” Dr. Brown said. “They start with demographics
...
health behaviors, then move into planning — who is affected, who are
...
stakeholders and what can be done.”
...
To support students along the way, Dr. Brown incorporated assignments like online discussion posts and reflections. These early activities help students practice summarizing material, exploring public health concepts and experimenting with AI tools in a structured way.
...
The key, Dr. Brown said, is allowing AI, but with clear limits. Early in the course, students are encouraged to use AI to summarize readings or explore basic public health concepts with detailed instructions and guardrails.
...
As the course progresses, AI use becomes more restricted. By the time students are connecting theories and designing interventions, they must rely on their own critical thinking.
...
By the final section, where students design an evaluation plan, AI is no longer allowed.
...
“There’s no shortcut there, because you have to explain and defend your decisions,” Dr. Brown said. “AI can give you the basics –– the definitions and the overview –– but the higher-level thinking, like analyzing, evaluating and applying, that’s where students grow.”
...
For Tagbor, it helped her understand how AI is changing research and writing from a broader perspective.
...
Dr. Brown and the team are now sharing their research with others in the hopes of inspiring more writing intensive public health courses to successfully use AI tools and decrease student intimidation. The group has published a paper on the subject in the Journal of Health Administration Education, and Dr. Brown presented her research through ODUGlobal’s Research Institute for Digital Innovation in Learning during the Spring 2026 semester.
...
Despite the promise of AI in helping students strengthen their skills, people remain the experts, especially in a field like public health. Whether addressing chronic disease, accessibility to green space or disparities in pregnancy outcomes, real-world public health work requires empathy, communication and trust, she said.
...
“AI can explain theories or design a program, but it can’t replace human connection,” Dr. Brown said. “You still need to talk to people in order to understand their fears and lived experiences. AI can’t do that.”

### Classroom Drowsiness Detection using
...
and alert mechanisms.
...
### Medical Video Captioning using Deep Learning and Transformer Models
...
This project focused on generating descriptive medical captions from MRI scan videos using deep learning and transformer architectures. I performed a comparative analysis using models like MobileNet and VGG16 for feature extraction, alongside a Transformer model for sequence generation. The MRI videos were segmented into 1-second frames, and visual features were extracted from each frame. These features were then paired with diagnostic captions sourced from hospital reports. The model was trained to map visual inputs to medical descriptions, and the final solution included a user interface to demonstrate automated video captioning for clinical applications. Show less

# Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist
...
Posted Jun 30, 2026
...
The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.
...
- Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Operations, along with product management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques.
...
- Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases.
...
- Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work.
...
- Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA.
...
- Work with production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance.
...
- Participate in other data science teams collaborating with your peers to support their projects.
...
- Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team.
...
- Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform.
...
- Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption.
...
- Qualifications
- PhD or Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.
...
- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
...
- 9+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
...
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
...
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
...
- Passion for collaboration, learn it all mindset and driving value with AI.
...
Date of posting: 1/14/2026

# Principal Data Scientist-Gen AI, Machine Learning (10042)

results.title: Jessica M.
highlights:
‣ Conducting computational research on rhetorical framing in factual vs. misinformative YouTube videos about the origins of Lyme disease ‣ Designed and executed a computational research study analyzing linguistic and rhetorical differences between factual and misinformative YouTube content ‣ Developed a reproducible methodology for video discovery and selection to reduce algorithmic bias, including defined engagement thresholds and validation criteria ‣ Collaborated with the Old Dominion University’s Biology Department’s Tick Research Lab to validate a dataset of videos to ensure scientific accuracy and contextual grounding ‣ Built and adapted Python scripts for transcript extraction, metadata parsing, and preprocessing in response to platform and API constraints ‣ Implemented NLP pipelines using Gensim, NLTK, and WordNet, including tokenization, stopword removal, lemmatization, and custom bigram detection ‣ Applied and tuned Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) models to analyze thematic structure and vocabulary patterns across content types ‣ Evaluated model outputs using coherence metrics, keyword inspection, word clouds, and interactive visualizations (PyLDAvis) ‣ Identified recurring rhetorical patterns, including emotional repetition and narrative framing, that distinguished misinformative content from factual explanations ‣ Interpreted computational findings in the context of existing misinformation and engagement research to inform future modeling directions ‣ Designed future research phases incorporating sentiment analysis, user engagement classification, and linguistic feature annotation ‣ Authored a 28-page report detailing methodology, dataset construction, topic modeling results, limitations, and recommended future directions
...
‣ Selected as 1 of 10 students from a pool of 142 applicants for a prestigious NSF-funded research internship focused on disinformation detection and analytics ‣ Conducted independent research on misinformation in the Venezuela-Guyana border dispute and its impact on sentiment toward Venezuelan migrants ‣ Created custom Python scripts using the YouTube Data API to collect and structure a dataset of 21,000+ comments ‣ Cleaned and preprocessed data using Pandas by removing punctuation, special characters, and stopwords, converting text to lowercase, and translating ~2,000 non-English comments using a custom-built ChatGPT-4o translation tool ‣ Developed a sentiment analysis program with TextBlob, expanding a keyword list from 20 to 392 terms to grow the relevant dataset from 800 to 6,201 comments ‣ Conducted exploratory data analysis to identify key themes and trends, cross-referencing findings with reputable news sources for validation ‣ Created visualizations (bar charts, line graphs, word clouds, sentiment trend graphs) to illustrate findings and support analytical interpretation ‣ Documented progress through detailed weekly logs and authored a final report outlining methodology, results, and recommendations for future work ‣ Delivered a final research presentation to an audience of 30+ doctoral staff and students; invited to continue research beyond summer based on performance

At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.

Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
